commit.message:
fix: truncate long player names to fit mDNS 63-byte label limit (#115)

Long speaker names like '[AV] Samsung Soundbar M360 M-Series @ asus-laptop-ubuntu'
caused zeroconf.BadTypeInNameException when the fallback player_id (no MAC)
exceeded the 63-byte DNS label limit.

Now truncates with a stable SHA-256 hash suffix for uniqueness when the
sendspin-{name} candidate exceeds 63 bytes.

commit.message:
fix: indexer never writes files, vault-wide block ID uniqueness

Remove block ID assignment from the indexer thread — it was writing
directly to disk via atomic_write(), bypassing the self-write hash
guard and causing false reload prompts.

Block IDs are now assigned only when a file is open in the editor,
via ensure_block_ids() on Insert→Normal transitions. Files without
IDs are indexed as-is.

Also adds vault-wide collision avoidance:
- BloomEditor.known_block_ids cache, refreshed on IndexComplete
- compute_block_id_assignments() accepts optional known IDs set
- New IDs inserted into cache immediately on assignment

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: suppress false reload prompts during undo/redo

Two-part fix for file watcher echoing our own saves back as external
changes:

1. Content-hash self-write detection: hash every save, suppress watcher
   events whose disk content matches a stored hash (bounded deque of 8).

2. Undo/redo autosave debounce: set 300ms deadline instead of saving
   immediately, coalescing rapid undo sequences into a single write.

commit.message:
feat: undo delta persistence with keyframe support

Store EditDelta (offset, delete_len, insert_text) per undo node instead
of full buffer content. Every 50th node stores a keyframe (full text)
bounding chain length. BFS restore on load reconstructs Ropey structural
sharing. Backward compatible with existing all-content format.

Storage: ~25 MB → ~540 KB for reference vault (20 pages × 500 nodes).

Also adds lab design docs for block ID metadata and undo delta stress
testing (18 correctness scenarios, 5 failure modes).

Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (#12)

* Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE

Implement the full deposit write workflow, all behind an explicit opt-in:

  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true   (or '1') enables write tools
  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=false  (default) — write tools not registered

## New tools (only registered when write is enabled)

Deposition CRUD:
  create_deposition   POST /api/deposit/depositions
  get_deposition      GET  /api/deposit/depositions/:id
  update_deposition   PUT  /api/deposit/depositions/:id  (metadata JSON)
  delete_deposition   DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id (drafts only)

File management:
  upload_file             PUT to bucket URL; accepts utf-8 or base64 content
  delete_deposition_file  DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id/files/:file_id

Deposition actions:
  publish_deposition   .../actions/publish
  edit_deposition      .../actions/edit  (unlock published record)
  discard_deposition   .../actions/discard
  new_version          .../actions/newversion

## Safety design

- Write tools are not registered at all when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE is false,
  so AI agents never see them unless the operator has opted in.
- Each handler also checks the flag independently (defence-in-depth).
- All write methods call requireAuth() — clear error if key is missing.
- docker-compose.yml exposes ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (default: false).

## Tests (test/zenodo-write.test.ts, 19 tests)

- Gate: write tools absent in tool list when disabled
- Gate: write tools present when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true
- Read-only tools still present when write is enabled
- 10 defence-in-depth handler tests (each write tool returns isError when flag off)
- 2 not-authenticated tests (write enabled, no API key)
- 4 live sandbox round-trip tests (create → upload → update → delete),
  activated only when ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY is set

CI: passes ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY secret and sets ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE
accordingly.

38/38 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update locale strings (#8339)

This PR updates the locale translation strings extracted from source
code.

## Triggered By
Commit: `3fbe7208901ec5992128e85901ecc43d5f368f8b`
Message: fix: Donation fund Active field always shows "Yes" and changes
not retained (#8319)

The Donation Fund editor showed all funds as Active="Yes" regardless of
saved state, and setting a fund to "No" had no effect. Three bugs in
`src/DonationFundEditor.php` were responsible.

## Bugs fixed

- **Reading — `boolval('false') === true`**: The `fun_Active` column
stores the literal strings `'true'`/`'false'` in an
`enum('true','false')` DB column. `boolval()` treats any non-empty
string as truthy, so `'false'` was read back as active.
  ```php
  // Before — always true for stored string 'false'
  $aActiveFields[$row] = boolval($donation->getActive());

  // After
  $aActiveFields[$row] = $donation->getActive() === 'true';
  ```

- **Writing — PHP bool stored into `enum('true','false')` column**:
`setActive()` received a PHP boolean, which Propel casts to `'1'`/`''` —
neither is a valid enum value, so MySQL silently rejected the write.
  ```php
  // Before — stores invalid enum value
  $donation->setActive($_POST[$iFieldID . 'active'] == 1);

  // After
$isActive = isset($_POST[$iFieldID . 'active']) && $_POST[$iFieldID .
'active'] == 1;
  $donation->setActive($isActive ? 'true' : 'false');
  ```

- **Delete action — assignment `=` instead of comparison `==`**: `if
($sAction = 'delete' && strlen($sFund) > 0)` overwrote `$sAction` with a
boolean, meaning any request with a `Fund` query param (regardless of
`Action`) would trigger a deletion.
  ```php
  // Before
  if ($sAction = 'delete' && strlen($sFund) > 0) {
  // After
  if ($sAction == 'delete' && strlen($sFund) > 0) {
  ```

fix: improve pool diagnostics and serialize db verification tests (#159)

## What type of PR is this?

- [x] fix (bug fix)
- [x] test (adding or updating tests)

## Which issue(s) this PR fixes

Fixes #

## What this PR does / why we need it

This PR improves database connection pool diagnostics and fixes flaky DB
verification tests.

On the diagnostics side, it makes main-pool logging more accurate:
- distinguish `empty` (no established connections) from `saturated` (all
established connections busy)
- avoid noisy repeated warnings by logging state transitions and
throttled repeats
- treat idle-drained empty pools as informational, but still emit
warnings/errors when recent real connectivity anomalies occurred
- expose additional pool-health and connection-anomaly metrics in
`/metrics`

On the test side, it serializes `api_db_verify.rs` because those
integration tests share a real database and can interfere with each
other when run concurrently, causing flaky failures unrelated to product
behavior.

### Key changes

- `memoria-storage/src/store.rs`
  - add structured pool health state tracking
- improve monitor logs for `healthy` / `high_utilization` / `saturated`
/ `empty`
- record and surface recent connectivity anomalies such as pool timeout
/ pool closed / IO / TLS / protocol / too-many-connections

- `memoria-api/src/routes/metrics.rs`
  - add main-pool active/state/duration/anomaly metrics
  - add auth-pool active metric

- `memoria-api/tests/api_db_verify.rs`
  - mark DB-sharing integration tests as `#[serial]`

- `memoria-api/Cargo.toml`
  - add `serial_test` dev dependency

### Validation

- `cargo test -p memoria-storage --lib -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p memoria-api --test pool_isolation -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p memoria-api --test api_db_verify -- --nocapture`
- `make check`

commit.message:
PMI: replace INT 31h with direct GDT patching for ~20x faster CS expansion

The previous fix used DPMI INT 31h AX=0008h inline before every PMI
call to re-expand CS.limit to 4GB.  While correct, INT 31h goes through
PMODETSR's full DPMI dispatcher (context save/restore, descriptor table
lookup, privilege checks) — hundreds of cycles per call.  This caused
a ~100 MB/s drop in blit throughput (1000 → 900 MB/s).

Replace with direct GDT descriptor byte-patching:
  cli
  mov  g_cs_gdt_ptr, %edi    ; cached near ptr to CS GDT entry
  movw $0xFFFF, (%edi)       ; limit[0:15] = 0xFFFF
  orb  $0x8F, 6(%edi)        ; G=1, limit[16:19]=0xF → 4GB
  push %cs                    ; reload CS hidden descriptor cache
  push $label                ;   via far return (LRET)
  lret                        ;
  call *%esi                  ; PMI call with 4GB CS limit
  sti

This is just 3 memory writes + LRET vs a full software interrupt.
The GDT entry pointer is computed once during expand_cs_to_4gb() and
cached in g_cs_gdt_ptr for all subsequent _pmi_call4() invocations.

Applied to all three PMI-capable DJGPP demos:
- PLASMA/VGA.C
- DUCKHUNT/DUCKHUNT.C
- DUCKHUNT/DUCKHUNTHC.C

commit.message:
Fix PMI CS.limit crash: inline DPMI 0008h re-expansion before every call *esi

PMODETSR reverts CS.limit to the original executable size after every
DPMI real-mode simulation (kbhit/getch via INT 16h reflection, etc.).
Pre-loop expand_cs_to_4gb() succeeded but kbhit() at the top of the
render loop immediately reset CS.limit back before the first
pmi_set_display_start() call, causing a #GP at call *%%esi.

Fix: embed DPMI 0008h (Set Segment Limit) inline in _pmi_call4() and
_pmi_call4_ebx() assembly, immediately before 'call *%%esi', gated by
CLI/STI so no IRQ can trigger a V86 switch in the gap:

  cli
  push eax/ebx/ecx/edx        ; save PMI register arguments
  mov  cs, bx                 ; DPMI 0008h: expand CS to 4 GB
  mov  0xffff, ecx/edx        ; CX:DX = limit = 0xFFFFFFFF
  mov  0x0008, ax
  int  0x31
  pop  edx/ecx/ebx/eax        ; restore PMI arguments
  call *%%esi                 ; CS.limit is guaranteed 4 GB here
  sti

Applied to all three PMI-capable DJGPP demos:
- PLASMA/VGA.C: _pmi_call4 and _pmi_call4_ebx
- DUCKHUNT/DUCKHUNT.C: same (8bpp)
- DUCKHUNT/DUCKHUNTHC.C: same (16bpp highcolor)

Also added CS expansion probe during PMI init in both DUCKHUNT files:
if DPMI 0008h cannot expand CS (e.g. CWSDPR0 with LDT selectors),
PMI is disabled gracefully with a diagnostic message instead of
crashing later.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
